sections.title: Israel / US vs Iran — opening moves

claims.text: Israel breaks ceasefire and strikes Iran
claims.source: Major strikes June 13, 2025 (12-Day War); Operation Epic Fury Feb 28, 2026 — Wikipedia / Britannica

claims.text: Trump responds with B-2 bunker busters on nuclear sites
claims.source: B-2 Spirit bombers + GBU-57 MOPs on Fordow & other nuclear sites — CRS Report / Congress.gov

claims.text: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz
claims.source: IRGC closed Hormuz to US/allied ships March 2-4, 2026; oil traffic down ~90% — Wikipedia / CRS

claims.text: Iran hits all Gulf oil fields and US bases; blocks Bab el-Mandeb
claims.source: Saudi Ras Tanura & Al-Udeid struck; all 6 GCC nations hit. Bab el-Mandeb: Houthi ceasefire in effect — ACLED / NBC News

updates.text: Reports confirm attacks on the US Navy Fifth Fleet headquarters and disruptions in the Red Sea, but no confirmation of Iran directly hitting all Gulf oil fields.
An attack on the US Navy Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain occurred, and Hormuz disruption is noted, but claims of Iran hitting *all* Gulf oil fields and blocking Bab el-Mandeb remain unconfirmed.

Reports indicate Hormuz disruption impacting oil markets, aligning with the claim of blocking Bab el-Mandeb. No confirmation of direct attacks on Gulf oil fields or US bases is present in the headlines provided.

claims.text: Oil prices skyrocket; blackouts in developing world
claims.source: Brent crude peaked at $126/bbl; Goldman Sachs warns elevated prices through 2027 — Bloomberg / Reuters

claims.text: US sends 3 aircraft carriers
claims.source: USS Abraham Lincoln, Gerald R. Ford, George H.W. Bush all deployed by March 7, 2026. Ford to Crete for repairs — Stars & Stripes

claims.text: At least 2 carriers sunk by Iran, Russia, and China using suicide tactics
claims.source: Iran claimed damage to USS Abraham Lincoln; US denied. No carrier sunk since WWII — NBC News / National Interest
keywords: carrier battle group attack Middle East

claims.text: US tries amphibious invasion of Iran — first attempt fails with massive casualties
claims.source: Conflict has been air/naval only — CENTCOM Operation Epic Fury. No ground invasion yet.

updates.text: USS Boxer deployment suggests increased US naval presence in the region, but doesn't confirm an invasion attempt.
Headlines indicate increased US military presence and consideration of operations targeting Kharg Island, suggesting heightened tensions but no confirmed invasion or casualties.

claims.text: Second attempt succeeds, oil fields seized, advance halts
claims.source: No such event — first invasion attempt has not even occurred yet
keywords: US Iran ground operation oil field

updates.text: Headlines indicate a US military operation targeting Kharg Island began, but reports suggest occupation proved 'daunting,' failing to confirm successful seizure of oil fields.

claims.text: US civil war; anti-war bloc seizes DC with military support
claims.source: No evidence of any such event

claims.text: US splits in two and retreats from Middle East
claims.source: US expanding operations, not retreating — CENTCOM (March 2026)

claims.text: Turkey betrays NATO, joins Russia-Iran-China, attacks Israel
claims.source: Turkey condemned strikes; closed airspace to US ops. NATO intact. Has not attacked Israel.

updates.text: A missile alert at the US Incirlik base suggests heightened tensions and potential instability, aligning with the claim's premise of Turkey shifting allegiances, but doesn't confirm it.

Headlines indicate Turkey intercepted Iranian missiles and faces escalating tensions with Iran, raising concerns about its NATO alignment, but no formal alliance with Russia/China or attack on Israel is confirmed.

Recent headlines indicate heightened tensions between Turkey, Iran, and NATO, alongside concerns about Turkish airspace and missile defense capabilities. No definitive alliance with Russia or China is confirmed, nor is an attack on Israel.

claims.text: China invades Taiwan and Guam
claims.source: No invasion. China opposes war rhetorically, has not aided Iran materially.

updates.text: China raised defense budget 7%, escalated Taiwan rhetoric, improving amphibious capabilities. US arms deal to Taiwan suspended. ISW/AEI warn Iran war created Taiwan vulnerability window. Xi-Trump summit scheduled end of March.

claims.text: North Korea invades South Korea; Japan fails to stop it
claims.source: No invasion. NK condemned US-Israeli strikes, no direct military involvement.

updates.text: Kim oversaw tests from new naval destroyer; declared goal of nuclear-armed Navy. ~10 ballistic missiles fired March 14 during Freedom Shield exercises. Reports: US moving THAAD from South Korea to Middle East — creating defensive gap.

claims.text: India possibly flips sides
claims.source: India received Iranian Hormuz transit permission. Pragmatic neutrality — Al Jazeera (March 2026)

updates.text: Headlines explicitly state India has chosen to back Israel over Iran, indicating a shift in alignment. This contradicts the original claim of 'possibly flipping sides' and confirms a change in stance.

claims.text: Russia invades Poland and Baltics; links to Kaliningrad
claims.source: No such event. Russia discussing Hormuz energy cooperation with US — NBC News

claims.text: Finland and NATO invade Russia, take Saint Petersburg
claims.source: No such event

updates.text: While one headline mentions Finland preparing for an attack on Russia (attributed to Medvedev), the others focus on Finland's economic challenges, security talks, and preparedness strategies, not an invasion.

claims.text: Europe collapses into riots, famine, and civil wars
claims.source: Energy price pressure; Germany/Greece refused US Hormuz coalition — Al Jazeera (March 2026)
keywords: Europe famine energy crisis Iran war

claims.text: Latin America allies with China and Russia
claims.source: No significant shift. US removed Venezuela's Maduro January 2026 — Reuters

claims.text: US invaded from the south; advance halts at Colorado
claims.source: No such event

claims.text: 50-150 tactical nukes used high in atmosphere
claims.source: No nuclear weapons used — all available sources

claims.text: Tsar Bomb and two strategic nukes used in Europe
claims.source: No nuclear weapons used — all available sources

claims.text: Israel destroyed; Palestinians wiped with it
claims.source: Israel conducting ongoing strikes. Gaza ceasefire in effect — Times of Israel (March 2026)

claims.text: Air travel ends; crime and chaos everywhere
claims.source: 6+ countries closed airspace Feb 28; major airlines suspended Middle East routes. Not a global end.

updates.text: Significant flight cancellations and airspace closures across the Middle East due to escalating conflict indicate a severe disruption to air travel, but not a complete end globally.

Significant flight cancellations and suspensions are occurring due to the Middle East conflict, disrupting international air travel. However, 'air travel ends' is an overstatement; it's severely disrupted, not ceased entirely.
Significant flight cancellations and disruptions are occurring due to the Middle East conflict, but air travel hasn't entirely ceased. Reports indicate revised schedules and challenges for airlines.

claims.text: 3 weeks of nuclear events
claims.source: No nuclear weapons used — all available sources

claims.text: Fake alien invasion begins; MJ12 releases synthetic alien soldiers (PLFs)
claims.source: None of this has occurred

claims.text: PLFs hunt survivors; FEMA camps classify people as useful or undesirable
claims.source: None of this has occurred

claims.text: By 2027, 5 billion dead; one world government; Dome of the Rock destroyed
claims.source: None of this has occurred — UN population data

claims.text: Fake aliens defeated with help of real ones
claims.source: None of this has occurred

claims.text: One world government under MJ12 and the UN
claims.source: None of this has occurred

claims.text: Dome of the Rock destroyed
claims.source: Dome of the Rock still standing — all available sources
